commit 9ffcf507788297b691225ff2115c63c2c885aa91
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 13 15:17:05 2026 -0400

    fix(init): isolate git env to prevent hook-inherited GIT_DIR corruption
    
    When ddx init or tests are invoked from a git hook (lefthook pre-commit),
    the process inherits GIT_DIR from the hook context. Git commands that set
    Dir to a different directory still use the inherited GIT_DIR, causing those
    commands to operate on the hook's repository — corrupting .git/config
    (core.bare = true, stray core.worktree entries) and causing tests to
    misidentify non-git temp dirs as git repos.
    
    Changes:
    - Add gitEnvForDir() helper that strips GIT_DIR, GIT_INDEX_FILE,
      GIT_WORK_TREE, GIT_OBJECT_DIRECTORY from inherited environment
    - Apply gitEnvForDir to all git commands in ddx init (add, commit, config,
      validateGitRepo rev-parse check)
    - Add TestMain to testutils_test.go that unsets git env vars before
      running any tests — fixes systematic failure of tests that spawn git
      commands when run through lefthook pre-commit
    - Also fix initGit() and RunCommand() helpers in testutils_test.go
    - Restore .ddx/config.yaml, .gitignore, AGENTS.md mangled by spurious
      commit c0e43af (caused by a prior failed hook run before this fix)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

1	package git
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10	)
11	
12	// Command returns a prepared *exec.Cmd for a git invocation that honors
13	// cmd.Dir / -C <path> rather than whatever GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc.
14	// were set in the parent environment.
15	//
16	// Why this wrapper exists (ddx-aa8a5fb3):
17	// Git's repository-discovery rules mean that if GIT_DIR is set in the
18	// process environment, git uses THAT repo and ignores both -C <path> and
19	// the process's working directory for repository selection. ddx commands
20	// invoked from a git hook (lefthook pre-commit, pre-push, etc.) inherit
21	// the hook's GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / GIT_INDEX_FILE. Without stripping
22	// those, any exec.Command("git", ...) inside ddx mutates the hook's
23	// repository (the outer one), producing core.bare=true flips and stray
24	// core.worktree entries in .git/config.
25	//
26	// The wrapper strips exactly git's "local env vars" set (the vars git
27	// itself clears when spawning worktree subprocesses). This is the
28	// canonical set documented by `git rev-parse --local-env-vars`. It
29	// preserves everything else, including GIT_SSH_COMMAND, GIT_TRACE*,
30	// GIT_AUTHOR/COMMITTER_NAME/EMAIL, and custom debugging overrides.
31	//
32	// Dir must be an absolute or working-directory-relative path; it is set
33	// on the returned cmd so that when no GIT_* local vars are in the
34	// environment, git discovers the repo from Dir.
35	func Command(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
36		warnOnce.Do(maybeWarnGitDirLeaked)
37	
38		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...)
39		if dir != "" {
40			cmd.Dir = dir
41		}
42		cmd.Env = CleanEnv()
43		return cmd
44	}
45	
46	// CleanEnv returns os.Environ() with every git local-env-var removed.
47	// Exported so sibling packages (e.g. internal/agent) that spawn NON-git
48	// subprocesses inside a worktree can use the same scrub — those child
49	// processes often invoke git themselves and need the same protection.
50	func CleanEnv() []string {
51		vars := localEnvVars()
52		src := os.Environ()
53		out := make([]string, 0, len(src))
54		for _, kv := range src {
55			eq := strings.IndexByte(kv, '=')
56			if eq < 0 {
57				out = append(out, kv)
58				continue
59			}
60			if vars[kv[:eq]] {
61				continue
62			}
63			out = append(out, kv)
64		}
65		return out
66	}
67	
68	// localEnvVars returns the set of environment variable names that git
69	// treats as repository-local (i.e. scoped to the current git process's
70	// repository selection and cleared when git itself spawns subprocesses
71	// for other worktrees). Result is cached for process lifetime.
72	//
73	// We query `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` once at first use so we pick
74	// up any vars a newer git version adds. If that query fails (git not on
75	// PATH, or restricted environment), fall back to the documented set.
76	func localEnvVars() map[string]bool {
77		localEnvVarsOnce.Do(func() {
78			set := map[string]bool{}
79			// Use exec.Command directly here to avoid recursion through
80			// Command(). This runs with inherited env on purpose: even if
81			// GIT_DIR is set, `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` is a static
82			// list of variable NAMES (not their values), so inherited env
83			// doesn't affect the output.
84			out, err := exec.Command("git", "rev-parse", "--local-env-vars").Output()
85			if err == nil {
86				for _, name := range strings.Fields(string(out)) {
87					if name != "" {
88						set[name] = true
89					}
90				}
91			}
92			// Fallback / supplement: the canonical set from git's source
93			// (environment.c local_repo_env[]). Kept inline so ddx remains
94			// functional even if `git rev-parse` is unavailable.
95			for _, name := range []string{
96				"GIT_DIR",
97				"GIT_WORK_TREE",
98				"GIT_INDEX_FILE",
99				"GIT_COMMON_DIR",
100				"GIT_OBJECT_DIRECTORY",
101				"GIT_ALTERNATE_OBJECT_DIRECTORIES",
102				"GIT_PREFIX",
103				"GIT_INTERNAL_GETTEXT_SH_SCHEME",
104				"GIT_SHALLOW_FILE",
105				"GIT_NO_REPLACE_OBJECTS",
106				"GIT_REPLACE_REF_BASE",
107				"GIT_CONFIG",
108				"GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL",
109				"GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM",
110				"GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS",
111				"GIT_CONFIG_COUNT",
112				"GIT_INDEX_VERSION",
113				"GIT_LITERAL_PATHSPECS",
114				"GIT_GLOB_PATHSPECS",
115				"GIT_NOGLOB_PATHSPECS",
116				"GIT_ICASE_PATHSPECS",
117				"GIT_QUARANTINE_PATH",
118				"GIT_INTERNAL_NEW_OBJECT_HOOK",
119				"GIT_IMPLICIT_WORK_TREE",
120			} {
121				set[name] = true
122			}
123			localEnvVarsCache = set
124		})
125		return localEnvVarsCache
126	}
127	
128	var (
129		localEnvVarsOnce  sync.Once
130		localEnvVarsCache map[string]bool
131	
132		warnOnce sync.Once
133	)
134	
135	// maybeWarnGitDirLeaked emits a one-time stderr diagnostic when the
136	// parent environment has GIT_DIR set. This is the canonical smoke signal
137	// for hook-inherited env contamination; surfacing it during normal runs
138	// helps catch upstream leaks we haven't yet wrapped.
139	func maybeWarnGitDirLeaked() {
140		if v := os.Getenv("GIT_DIR"); v != "" {
141			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
142				"ddx git wrapper: stripped inherited GIT_DIR=%q (likely hook context). "+
143					"This is diagnostic; operation proceeds against cmd.Dir instead.\n", v)
144		}
145	}

18	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	// Git helpers
20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}

65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")

89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())

94			b := &bead.Bead{

100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))

104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")

123	// Executor helpers
124	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}
146	
147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}

101	// RealOrchestratorGitOps implements OrchestratorGitOps via os/exec git commands.
102	type RealOrchestratorGitOps struct{}
103	
104	// UpdateRef updates a git ref to point at sha.
105	func (r *RealOrchestratorGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
106		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
107		if err != nil {
108			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
109		}
110		return nil

112	
113	// BeadLandingOptions controls how the orchestrator lands a completed worker result.
114	type BeadLandingOptions struct {
115		// NoMerge skips the land step and preserves the result under
116		// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... instead.
117		NoMerge bool
118	
119		// WtPath is the path to a worktree checked out at ResultRev, used for gate
120		// evaluation. When empty, gate evaluation is skipped (the worktree has
121		// typically been cleaned up by the time the orchestrator runs).
122		WtPath string
123	
124		// GovernIDs are the governing artifact IDs to use for gate evaluation.
125		// Only used when WtPath is non-empty. Typically extracted from the worker's
126		// manifest artifact via ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest.
127		GovernIDs []string
128	
129		// ChecksArtifactPath is the absolute path to write checks.json. Optional.
130		ChecksArtifactPath string
131		// ChecksArtifactRel is the relative path stored in the result for checks.json.
132		ChecksArtifactRel string
133	
134		// LandingAdvancer, when non-nil, replaces the old in-process Merge step
135		// with the coordinator-pattern Land() call. The callback is expected to
136		// run fetch → (ff or merge) → push serialized against other submissions
137		// for the same projectRoot. When nil, LandBeadResult falls back to
138		// preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/...
139		// rather than touching the target branch.
140		LandingAdvancer func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error)
141	}
142	
143	// BeadLandingResult records the outcome of the orchestrator's landing step.
144	type BeadLandingResult struct {
145		// Outcome is one of: "merged", "preserved", "no-changes".
146		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
147		// Reason is a human-readable explanation of the outcome.
148		Reason string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
149		// PreserveRef is set when the result was preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/...
150		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
151		// GateResults holds the outcome of each required execution gate.
152		GateResults []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
153		// RequiredExecSummary is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
154		RequiredExecSummary string `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
155		// ChecksFile is the relative path to checks.json when gate results were written.
156		ChecksFile string `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
157		// RatchetEvidence aggregates every ratchet-evaluated gate's evidence for
158		// the attempt. Surfaced separately from GateResults so HELIX and other
159		// consumers can tell apart ratchet outcomes from generic gate failures.
160		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
161		// RatchetSummary is "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
162		RatchetSummary string `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
163	}
164	
165	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
166	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
167	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
168	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
169	//
170	// Outcome rules:
171	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
172	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
173	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
174	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
175	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
176	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
177		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
178	
179		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
180		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
181		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
182		if res.ExitCode != 0 && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
183			landing.Outcome = "error"
184			switch {
185			case res.Reason != "":
186				landing.Reason = res.Reason
187			case res.Error != "":
188				landing.Reason = res.Error
189			default:
190				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
191			}
192			return landing, nil
193		}
194	
195		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
196		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
197			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
198			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
199				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
200			} else {
201				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
202			}
203			return landing, nil
204		}
205	
206		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
207		if res.ExitCode != 0 {
208			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
209			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
210				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
211			}
212			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
213			landing.PreserveRef = ref
214			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
215			return landing, nil
216		}
217	
218		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
219		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
220		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting
221		// through the coordinator. It mutates res.GateResults / RequiredExecSummary
222		// / RatchetEvidence / RatchetSummary / ChecksFile in place; we mirror the
223		// outputs onto landing so ApplyLandingToResult can copy them back later
224		// without losing the existing landing.* contract.
225		anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gateErr := EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(
226			opts.WtPath, opts.GovernIDs, res, projectRoot,
227			opts.ChecksArtifactPath, opts.ChecksArtifactRel,
228		)
229		if gateErr != nil {
230			return nil, gateErr
231		}
232		gateResults := res.GateResults
233		landing.GateResults = gateResults
234		landing.RequiredExecSummary = res.RequiredExecSummary
235		landing.RatchetEvidence = res.RatchetEvidence
236		landing.RatchetSummary = res.RatchetSummary
237		landing.ChecksFile = res.ChecksFile
238	
239		// Gate failed: preserve instead of merging. Ratchet misses get a
240		// dedicated reason so status/failure_mode classifiers can distinguish
241		// them from generic command failures.
242		if anyGateFailed {
243			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
244			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
245				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
246			}
247			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
248			landing.PreserveRef = ref
249			if anyRatchetFailed {
250				landing.Reason = RatchetPreserveReason
251			} else {
252				landing.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
253			}
254			return landing, nil
255		}
256	
257		// --no-merge: preserve unconditionally.
258		if opts.NoMerge {
259			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
260			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
261				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
262			}
263			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
264			landing.PreserveRef = ref
265			landing.Reason = "--no-merge specified"
266			return landing, nil
267		}
268	
269		// Default: land the worker's commits on the target branch. When a
